records.ety: [L. consceratus, p. p. of conscerare to conscerate; con- + sacrare to consecrate, sacer sacred. See Sacred.]

quotes: They were assembled in that consecrate place.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Consecrated; p. pr. & vb. n. Consecrating.]

defs: To make, or declare to be, sacred; to appropriate to sacred uses; to set apart, dedicate, or devote, to the service or worship of God; as, to consecrate a church; to give (one's self) unreservedly, as to the service of God.
quotes: One day in the week is . . . consecrated to a holy rest.

defs: To set apart to a sacred office; as, to consecrate a bishop.
quotes: Thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

defs: To canonize; to exalt to the rank of a saint; to enroll among the gods, as a Roman emperor.
To render venerable or revered; to hallow; to dignify; as, rules or principles consecrated by time.
